key_strengths: Strong rapport — natural small talk, name coincidence, relaxed opener
Rich family context surfaced — blended family, birth mum story, ADHD/ODD, husband away
Decent program pivot — linked 'not carrying it all on your own' to her stated pain
Good objection isolation — tested 'if Anthony said no, would you still go?' → got YES
Tayla mentioned potentially enrolling second son Chase too
critical_misses: EMOTIONAL BOMBS WALKED PAST: Birth mum abandonment, resentment, brick wall — zero cycling
Program mention too early — in first 90 seconds of frame-setting
No $111 close on the day — Tayla asked 'if I don't do the 111 today, what does that look like?' and Anthony let her off
Partner objection accepted too passively — no attempt to bridge the 4-day delay
No GREEN labels — all labels were generic, never used her exact words back

model_pain_stack.surface: Johnny cried all day at school, ADHD/ODD diagnosis, strangled little brother, codependent girlfriend at 12
model_pain_stack.impact: Communication completely broken — 'brick wall 90% of the time.' All hats worn alone when husband works away. No consistent male role model outside dad.
model_pain_stack.emotional: 'I'm starting to resent him and I hate that.' Fears he'll fill the void with drugs or wrong crowd. Feels like she's failing him.
model_pain_stack.deepest_fear: He already doesn't have his actual mum. The birth mum abandonment runs through everything. Never named by Anthony.

calls.one_drill: EMOTIONAL BOMB CYCLING DRILL: Pull this transcript. Find the 5 bombs (birth mum, resentment, brick wall, strangulation, 'failing him'). PAUSE. 'Tell me more.' 'When you feel that, what does it bring up for you?' Label with her exact words. Don't move on until 2+ mins.
calls.one_line: The hardest part wasn't what she said — it was what she dropped and you walked past. The birth mum story wasn't background. That WAS the whole call.

critical_misses: No recording available — call cannot be scored
